course_description:
This course will provide students with an overview of the challenges around caring for mothers and babies with a critical illness, including ways to prevent the devastating outcomes of critical illness in pregnancy, labour and the puerperium. The role of the midwife in early recognition of critical illness of childbearing women and identification of the need for a rapid response and/or timely referral to the multidisciplinary healthcare team to achieve safe, high quality care based on current evidence will be explored, together with the need for a knowledgeable and skilled multidisciplinary team who is well rehearsed in the treatment of maternal and neonatal emergency response.

This course is designed for second year Bachelor of Midwifery and third year Bachelor of Nursing Bachelor of Midwifery students. The course incorporates theory-based learning through lectures, and Inquiry Based Learning (IBL). The IBL sessions are designed to significantly aid student learning and critical thinking with oral assessments incorporated throughout the semester, presented face to face. Please note assessment in this course will be recorded via Zoom for marking and moderation purposes. Lectures will also be presented face to face and attendance is a course expectation. Lectures will also be recorded for review and study purposes. The course is held over 13 weeks and the educational focus is on developing an in-depth knowledge and understanding of managing a woman and neonateﾠwith critical illness in the peripartum period.

Additional timetable information
The Inquiry Based Learning sessions are down in the published timetable as tutorials.
course_aims: This course aims to provide students with the knowledge and skills required to care for women and neonates experiencing critical illness. Students will consolidate content learned in MIDW2102/MIDW2108 and continue to develop woman centred midwifery care skills needed to advance midwifery practice. Students will achieve this through attendance at, and participation in, lectures, and inquiry-based learning tutorials.

learning_outcomes.description: Critically examine the significance of consultation, referral and a multidisciplinary approach to the care of women and neonates with critical illness in pregnancy labour and the puerperium, including rural and remote settings.
Analyse the aetiology, management and apply skills including monitoring, assessment and care of the woman and baby for a range of emergency situations in pregnancy, labour, birth and the puerperium.

Using contemporary and appropriate evidence, critically review the pathophysiology of, diagnosis, and evidence for treatment of, and expected outcomes associated with critical illness and life-threatening emergencies of significance to midwifery practice.

Analyse and critically review the contemporary and relevant evidence used to inform midwifery care of women with a critical illness or life-threatening emergency, with a particular focus on the use of digital health technology to improve health outcomes.

Advocate culturally responsive strategies to support women and their families as they are challenged by and deal with the physical and psychological complications of a critical illness or a life-threatening emergency in pregnancy, labour, birth and the puerperium.

assessment_summary.title: Oral Presentation
assessment_summary.due_date: 14/08/2025 - 31/10/2025; Please submit your Oral Presentation via the link provided in the assessment folder of the Blackboard course on the day of your scheduled presentation.

assessment_details.title: Oral Presentation
assessment_details.due_date: 14/08/2025 - 31/10/2025 Please submit your Oral Presentation via the link provided in the assessment folder of the Blackboard course on the day of your scheduled presentation.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific, Peer assessed. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Your individual oral presentation topic will be generated as a course identified learning outcome or directly as a critical issue from the prior IBL session. Presentations from the previous IBL session will take place BEFORE the next IBL session and presented in class. Your 15-minute oral presentation will compose up to 40 marks for the subject. Please note presentations will be recorded for marking purposes in alignment with UQ policies and procedures. Time length : Your presentation must be within +/- 10% of the allocated time. A time limit outside this 10% will result in 10% reduction of the total mark available. Reference system: Slides must be referenced throughout using APA 7th edition or Vancouver. Header : Student ID (eight digits), Course Code AI and Academic Integrity: This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Please ensure that you complete and upload the AI assessment coversheet when you submit your assessment. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
This piece of assessment is to be submitted in the course Blackboard via the provided assessment link on the day of your scheduled presentation. By submitting your presentation, you are certifying that it is your original work and that where you have used the ideas or writing of other authors this has been acknowledged according to accepted academic guidelines.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 14 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Applying for an extension
Extension requests are submitted online via my.UQ. Extension requests received in any other way will not be approved.
Extension guidelines
Extension requests should be submitted prior to the assessment deadline. Requests for extensions received after the assessment item submission due date may not be approved. Late requests must include evidence of the reasons for the late request as per the guidelines in my.UQ . The extension granted should be in proportion to the period of illness or disruption caused by the exceptional circumstances and will typically be no more than the specified maximum extension length for each assessment. A maximum of three assessment requests can be submitted for each assessment, and the third request must include an Assessment Management Plan. Students who have been ill or unable to attend class for more than 4 weeks in a semester are advised to carefully consider whether they are capable of successfully completing their courses and may need to consider applying for removal of course . Students who have a disability or a chronic medical condition (including a mental health condition) are encouraged to meet with a Disability Advisor within Student Services to request a Student Access Plan (SAP) with a clause on extension requests. Students who have an SAP can apply for a 7-day extension.
Outcome of application
Extension requests are generally processed within 2 working days.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Online invigilated on campus exam
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This assessment will examine the learning objectives of the MIDW2105 course content. Questions will be derived from lecture content, readings, and provided course content. The exam will be, online invigilated on campus in this course using the Inspera Assessment Platform. Inspera Assessment provides additional functionality to complement the existing suite of digital learning tools at UQ with a greater range of question types and accessibility provisions. Further details, including an Inspera demo test to familiarise yourself with the functionality, and where to go for support can be found in the course companion Blackboard site. Criteria & Marking: UQ Students: Please access the profile from Learn.UQ or mySI-net to access the marking criteria for this assessment. Read full task description Hide full task description

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13 (28 Jul - 02 Nov)
learning_activities.topic: Inquiry based learning workshops (IBL) Inquiry Based Learning is a mode of instruction which allows the students to take responsibility for their own learning. Students are required to attend tutorials and to participate in the discussion and generation of learning needs from a particular case based scenario. These sessions will be held on the same weeks as the lectures. Attendance is highly recommended.(See timetable for location).

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13 (28 Jul - 02 Nov)
learning_activities.topic: On-line learning self directed Students are required to work through the on-line learning content of the course. Engaging in course readings, quizzes and learning activities

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13 (28 Jul - 02 Nov)
learning_activities.topic: Lecture Series In this course clinical experts in their respective fields ( Specialist Consultant Obstetricians, Clinical Specialist Midwives) will present the face to face lecture series, lectures will be recorded for review and study purposes however, attendance is highly recommended (See timetable for location).
